messages.content: Which two boundaries are guaranteed to be lossless when migrating from mpg to scrt?
The palace file format (on‑disk JSON) and the agent‑json envelope.
Does scrt keep the environment variable MPG_MIND_PALACE when migrating?
Yes, scrt keeps the MPG_MIND_PALACE env var unchanged.
Find all occurrences of the `scrt_similar` tool description in the source files under the `src/` directory.

```tool_code
scrt_search(pattern="scrt_similar", in=["src/"], effort="normal")
```
Create a mind‑palace stash named `migration-boundary` that records the two lossless boundaries described in the passage.

```tool_code
scrt_stash(name="migration-boundary", note="Lossless migration boundaries: palace file format and agent-json envelope", tags=["migration","boundary"], ttl="7d")
```
Retrieve the stash `migration-boundary` with its captured node bodies to review what was stored.

```tool_code
scrt_get_stash(name="migration-boundary", with_nodes=true)
```
Generate a JSON formatted palace file for the node with id 42 using scrt.
scrt --format json node 42
Run the scrt_similar tool on the source file src/auth/login.ts to find similar nodes.
scrt scrt_similar src/auth/login.ts
Retrieve the agent-json envelope for node id 7 in the current palace.
scrt agent-json 7
What exit code does scrt return when it encounters bad arguments?
Run the evolve subcommand with the 'init' argument.
evolve_cli::handle(&argv[1..]) is called, which processes the evolve subcommand.
Which flag should I use to display help information in scrt?
--help
Find all occurrences of the keyword 'scrt' in the main.rs file and return the matched lines.

```tool_code
scrt_search(pattern="\\bscrt\\b", in=["C:\\Users\\atooz\\Programming\\ai-utils-memory\\scrt-cli\\crates\\scrt-cli\\src\\main.rs"], effort="normal")
```
Save the search results from the previous query into a mind‑palace slot named 'scrt-main-search' with a note describing it.

```tool_code
scrt_stash(name="scrt-main-search", note="Occurrences of the keyword 'scrt' in main.rs, used for quick reference during debugging.")
```
List all existing mind‑palace slots that have been tagged with 'search'.

```tool_code
scrt_list_stashes(tag_filter=["search"])
```
Show help for the scrt command
scrt --help
Search for the word "rust" in a local directory with case-insensitive matching and output as markdown
scrt --in ./src --cmd "rust" --ignore-case --format markdown
Run an evolve subcommand to train a corpus located at /data/corpus
scrt evolve train /data/corpus
How do I set a maximum number of nodes when using the CLI?
Use the `--max-nodes <number>` flag, where `<number>` is the desired limit.
Parse the command line to extract the value for the `--mp-graph` option and its optional depth.
The parser looks for `--mp-graph`. It then clones the next argument as the graph name. If a following non‑flag argument exists, it parses that as an integer depth; otherwise the default depth is 3.
What values are allowed for the `--score` flag?
`--score` must be an integer between 1 and 10 inclusive.
Search for the regex pattern "mp_graph" in the Rust source files under src/args.rs and its subdirectories.

```tool_code
scrt_search(pattern="mp_graph", in=["C:\\Users\\atooz\\Programming\\ai-utils-memory\\scrt-cli\\crates\\scrt-cli\\src"], effort="normal")
```
Save the search results from the previous query into a mind‑palace stash named "mp-graph-search" with note describing its purpose and tags for future reference.

```tool_code
scrt_stash(name="mp-graph-search", note="Search results for the mp_graph flag in args.rs, used to understand graph depth parsing logic.", tags=["search","args.rs","mp-graph"])
```
Find stashes similar to "mp-graph-search" using full content similarity and rank the top 5 results.

```tool_code
scrt_similar(name="mp-graph-search", match="full", top=5)
```
Search for the pattern 'error' in all .log files under /var/log, ignoring case and only showing the first 10 matches.
scrt --pattern "error" --include-globs "*.log" --ignore-case --before 10
List all mind palace stashes that contain the tag 'project' and replace their names with a new prefix 'proj-'.
scrt --mp-list --mp-list-tags "project" --mp-stash-replace --mp-stash-name "proj-"
Compose two mind palace stashes named 'ideas' and 'notes', then drop the stash named 'old'.
scrt --mp-compose "ideas" "notes" --mp-drop "old"
How do I specify multiple paths for the `--mp-intersect` flag in the CLI?
After writing `--mp-intersect`, list each path separated by spaces or commas; the parser will split them and add to `mp_intersect`. For example: `--mp-intersect path1 path2,path3`.
What happens when I use the `--mp-link` flag without providing a note?
The parser reads three required arguments: `<from>`, `<to>`, and `<type>`. If no additional argument follows, the optional note remains None.
Search for all occurrences of the `--mp-intersect` flag in the CLI argument parser source.

```tool_code
scrt_search(pattern="--mp-intersect", in=["C:\\Users\\atooz\\Programming\\ai-utils-memory\\scrt-cli\\crates\\scrt-cli\\src\\args.rs"], effort="normal")
```
Store the search results from the previous query into a mind‑palace stash named `mp-intersect-usage` with a note describing its purpose.

```tool_code
scrt_stash(name="mp-intersect-usage", note="Occurrences of the --mp-intersect flag in the CLI parser source", tags=["cli","flags","search"])
```
Retrieve the newly created stash `mp-intersect-usage` including node bodies to review the context around each match.

```tool_code
scrt_get_stash(name="mp-intersect-usage", with_nodes=true)
```
Create a new mind palace stash named "Project Ideas" with the note "Brainstorming session" and tags tech and innovation.
scrt --mp-stash-name "Project Ideas" --mp-stash-note "Brainstorming session" --mp-stash-tags tech,innovation
Link the node "Idea A" to "Idea B" with a relationship type "influences" and add an optional note "High impact".
scrt --mp-link "Idea A" "Idea B" "influences" "High impact"
Compose two mind palace nodes "Node1" and "Node2", then intersect with a third node list "CommonNodes".
scrt --mp-compose Node1,Node2 --mp-intersect CommonNodes
How do I use the `--mp-link` flag in scrt-cli?
`--mp-link <from> <to> <type> [note]` where `<from>` and `<to>` are paths, `<type>` is a string, and an optional `[note]` can follow if it does not start with a dash.
Construct the command line to unlink two nodes using scrt-cli.
scrt-cli --mp-unlink <from> <to>
What is the valid range for the `--score` flag?
The value must be an integer between 1 and 10 inclusive.
Find all occurrences of the struct `RawArgs` definition in the Rust source tree under `scrt-cli/src/args.rs` and return the matched lines with file:line attribution.

```tool_code
scrt_search(pattern="\\bstruct\\s+RawArgs\\b", in=["C:\\Users\\atooz\\Programming\\ai-utils-memory\\scrt-cli\\crates\\scrt-cli\\src"], effort="normal")
```
Save the search results from the previous query into a mind‑palace slot named `raw-args-definition` with a note describing it as the Rust struct that holds raw CLI arguments.

```tool_code
scrt_stash(name="raw-args-definition", note="Definition of RawArgs struct in scrt-cli/src/args.rs", tags=["rust","cli-parser","struct"], replace=false)
```
List all existing mind‑palace slots that have the tag `rust` to see what other Rust‑related snippets are already stored.

```tool_code
scrt_list_stashes(tag_filter=["rust"])
```
Link stash 'alpha' to stash 'beta' as a dependency with note 'next step'.
scrt --mp-link alpha beta dependency next step
Remove the link from 'beta' to 'alpha'.
scrt --mp-unlink beta alpha
Show the mind palace graph for 'projectX' up to depth 5.
scrt --mp-graph projectX 5
How do I create a link between two mind palace nodes using the CLI?
Use the `--mp-link` flag followed by the source node, target node, relationship type, and an optional note. For example: `--mp-link fromNode toNode type "optional note"`.
What flag would I use to display a graph of mind palace nodes with a custom depth?
Use `--mp-graph <name> [depth]`. For example: `--mp-graph myGraph 5`.
Search for all occurrences of the `mp_link` flag handling in the CLI argument parser source file.

```tool_code
scrt_search(pattern="--mp-link", in=["C:\\Users\\atooz\\Programming\\ai-utils-memory\\scrt-cli\\crates\\scrt-cli\\src\\args.rs"], effort="normal")
```
Save the search results from the previous query into a mind‑palace stash named `cli-arg-parser-mp-link` with a note describing its purpose.

```tool_code
scrt_stash(name="cli-arg-parser-mp-link", note="Lines in args.rs that parse and handle the --mp-link flag for mind‑palace relationships.", tags=["args","mp-link"], replace=false)
```
Find stashes similar to `cli-arg-parser-mp-link` based on note content, returning only the top 2 most relevant stashes.

```tool_code
scrt_similar(name="cli-arg-parser-mp-link", match="note", top=2)
```
Search for the pattern 'error' in all .log files under /var/log, ignoring case and showing only the first 10 matches.
scrt --pattern "error" --in-paths /var/log/*.log --ignore-case --before 0 --after 10
Create a new mind palace stash named 'projectX' with tags 'development' and '2024', replace existing stash, and list all stashes containing the tag '2024'.
scrt --mp-stash-name projectX --mp-stash-tags development 2024 --mp-stash-replace --mp-list --mp-list-tags 2024
Link node 'nodeA' to node 'nodeB' with type 'depends_on', add a note, then generate a graph named 'dependencyGraph' up to depth 5.
scrt --mp-link nodeA nodeB depends_on "This is a dependency" --mp-graph dependencyGraph 5
What is the valid range for the `--score` flag in this CLI?
`--score` must be an integer between 1 and 10 inclusive.
Explain how to use the `--match` flag with its allowed values.

The `--match` flag accepts one of three string arguments: `note`, `full`, or `vector`. Depending on the value, it sets internal booleans:
- `note`: disables both full and vector matching.
- `full`: enables full matching and disables vector matching.
- `vector`: enables vector matching and disables full matching.
What happens if I provide a value greater than 100 to the `--link-threshold` flag?
The parser returns an error: "--link-threshold must be 0..100, got: {v}" and exits with code 2.
Search for the pattern "rust concurrency" in all .rs files under src/, ignoring case and showing only the first 5 matches, outputting JSON format.
scrt --pattern "rust concurrency" --ignore-case --in src/**/*.rs --before 0 --after 0 --max_nodes 5 --format json
Add a new mind palace stash named "project ideas" with the note "Brainstorm next features" and tags tech,ideas, and list all stashes that have the tag tech.
scrt --mp-stash-name "project ideas" --mp-stash-note "Brainstorm next features" --mp-stash-tags tech,ideas --mp-list --mp-list-tags tech
Compose two existing mind palace stashes named "frontend" and "backend", then drop the stash named "deprecated".
scrt --mp-compose frontend,backend --mp-drop deprecated
Create a new mind palace stash named "Ideas" with note "Brainstorm session" and tags "brainstorm","creative"
scrt --mp-stash Ideas --mp-stash-note "Brainstorm session" --mp-tag brainstorm --mp-tag creative
List all stashes that have the tag "research"
scrt --mp-list --mp-list-tag research
Compose a new mind palace from stashes "Ideas" and "Research", then drop the stash named "Draft"
scrt --mp-compose Ideas Research --mp-drop Draft
Which two migration boundaries are guaranteed to be lossless in scrt?
The palace file format (on‑disk JSON) and the agent‑json envelope.
What is the order of keys in a Node JSON object produced by scrt?
Keys are emitted in insertion/declaration order, matching the v0.x object literal.
What error is returned by add_relation if the source stash does not exist?
It returns an Err with the message "Unknown stash: {from}" where {from} is the missing stash name.
Add a directed relationship from stash 'A' to stash 'B' of type 'friend' with note 'colleague', using a Palace instance and a Clock implementation.
add_relation(&mut palace, &clock, "A", "B", "friend", "colleague")
How does traversal_graph determine inbound edges?
It iterates over all stashes and for each relation whose target equals the queried stash name, it creates a GraphNode with direction Inbound.
Which two boundaries are guaranteed to be lossless when migrating from mpg to scrt?
The palace file format (on‑disk JSON) and the agent‑json envelope.
What does the golden harness normalize before performing a byte‑by‑byte diff?
It normalizes the directory name from ".bmpg" to "scrt" and the prefix from "mpg_" to "scrt_" on both sides.
Generate a palace file from the current directory using scrt and output it in JSON format
scrt generate --mp-stash .mpg/mind-palace.json --format json
Run a search query with scrt and display results as agent-json envelope
scrt search "login" --format agent-json
Create a similar node list using the scrt_similar tool for nodes 1 through 5
scrt scrt_similar --nodes 1,2,3,4,5
How do I install the scrt binary from source?
Clone the repository, build with `cargo build --release`, then copy the resulting binary to a directory on your PATH, e.g., `cp target/release/scrt ~/.local/bin/`.
Verify that scrt is installed correctly.
Run `scrt --version`, `scrt --help`, and `scrt --ls` to confirm the binary works and lists searchable files in the current directory.
What command should I use to generate a tool specification for Claude API?
Use `scrt tool-spec --format anthropic` to produce a tool specification compatible with the Claude API.
Search for the term 'rust' in all files under src and list matching lines with line numbers.
scrt rust --in src/ --before 5 --after 5
Add a new mind palace stash named "project ideas" with note and tags, then compose it with the stash "existing notes".
scrt --mp-stash-name 'project ideas' --mp-stash-note 'Ideas for upcoming projects' --mp-stash-tags tech,planning --mp-compose existing notes
Retrieve and display the content of a stash named "meeting minutes" with its nodes included.
scrt --mp-get 'meeting minutes' --mp-get-with-nodes
How do I use scrt to filter the output of a command that contains error messages?
Use `scrt "FAIL|error TS" --cmd "gh run view --log 12345" --effort scan --clip 80 --max-tokens 2000`.
Create a stash named 'scan' that expires after 4 hours and tag it with 'scratch'.
scrt ... --mp-stash scan --mp-ttl 4h --mp-tag scratch
What flag do I use to rank stashes by lexical similarity using a weighted cosine vector?
Use `--match vector` with `scrt --mp-similar <stash>`.

Search for all occurrences of the regex pattern "TODO" in files that are present in both the rl-impl and rl-docs directories, then store the results in a mind‑palace stash named `todo-rl-intersect` with a TTL of 4 hours.

```tool_code
scrt_search(pattern="TODO", in=["rl-impl","rl-docs"], effort="scan", mp_stash="todo-rl-intersect", mp_ttl="4h")
```
Retrieve the stash `todo-rl-intersect` with its node bodies so that we can see the captured context for debugging.

```tool_code
scrt_get_stash(name="todo-rl-intersect", with_nodes=true)
```
Find stashes similar to `todo-rl-intersect` using the full‑text match, return only the top 5 most relevant ones.

```tool_code
scrt_similar(name="todo-rl-intersect", match="full", top=5)
```
Search for TODO comments in both rl-impl and rl-docs directories using the intersection lens.
scrt "TODO" --mp-intersect rl-impl rl-docs
Filter error logs from a GitHub Actions run by scanning the command output with a maximum of 2000 tokens.
scrt "FAIL|error TS" --cmd "gh run view --log 12345" --effort scan --clip 80 --max-tokens 2000
Create a short-lived stash named session-logs for temporary data and then prune all stashes tagged as scratch.
scrt "session logs" --mp-stash session-logs --mp-ttl 4h --mp-tag scan && scrt --mp-prune-tag scan
How do I perform a token-budgeted search for the term "TODO" in the src/ directory using scrt?
Run `scrt "TODO" --in src/`.
Generate a command that finds stashes similar to 'auth-login' using vector matching and returns only the top 5 results with a score threshold of 8.
scrt --mp-similar auth-login --match vector --score 8 --top 5
What flag can I use to suppress link suggestions when stashing with scrt?
Use the `--no-suggest-links` flag.
Search for all TODO comments in the src directory with a normal effort level.

```tool_code
scrt_search(pattern="TODO", in=["src/"], effort="normal")
```
Save the results of searching for "rate.?limit" in src with a deep effort into a stash named rate-limiter, tagging it as auth and persistence.

```tool_code
scrt_stash(name="rate-limiter", note="Context nodes for rate limiting logic in src", tags=["auth","persistence"], replace=false)
```
Find stashes similar to the newly created rate-limiter stash using full matching and return only the top 3.

```tool_code
scrt_similar(name="rate-limiter", match="full", top=3)
```
Search for the phrase "TODO" in the src directory with default token budget.
scrt "TODO" --in src/
Find stashes similar to auth-login using vector matching and only show the top 3 results with a score threshold of 7.
scrt --mp-similar auth-login --match vector --score 7 --top 3
Link two existing stashes auth-test and auth-login with the relation see-also, suppressing link suggestions.
scrt --mp-link auth-test auth-login see-also --no-suggest-links
How do I add a link between two mind palace nodes using the CLI?
Use `--mp-link <from> <to> <type>` followed by an optional note. For example: `scrt-cli --mp-link node1 node2 relation "Optional note"`.
Parse the command line arguments for a mind palace graph depth of 5.
`--mp-graph <name> 5`
What flag removes a relationship between two nodes?
Use `--mp-unlink <from> <to>` to remove the link.
Search for the regex "\bRawArgs\b" in the src directory of the project.

```tool_code
scrt_search(pattern="\\bRawArgs\\b", in=["C:\\Users\\atooz\\Programming\\ai-utils-memory\\scrt-cli\\crates\\scrt-cli\\src"], effort="normal")
```
Save the results of that search into a mind‑palace stash called "raw-args-search" with note "Occurrences of RawArgs struct" and tags ["struct", "search"].

```tool_code
scrt_stash(name="raw-args-search", note="Occurrences of RawArgs struct", tags=["struct","search"])
```
Retrieve the stash "raw-args-search" with its node bodies to review the captured lines.

```tool_code
scrt_get_stash(name="raw-args-search", with_nodes=true)
```
Create a link between two mind palace nodes with an optional note.
scrt --mp-link nodeA nodeB relation "This is a note"
Generate a graph of the mind palace named 'myStash' up to depth 5.
scrt --mp-graph myStash 5
Compose two existing stashes into a new one called 'combined'.
scrt --mp-compose stash1 stash2 --mp-stash-name combined
How do I install the scrt binary from source?
Clone the repository, build with `cargo build --release`, then copy the resulting `target/release/scrt` to a directory on your PATH.
Verify that scrt is correctly installed and can list files in the current working directory.

# From the terminal
scrt --ls
What command should I run to generate a Gemini extension tool specification?
Run `scrt tool-spec --format gemini > scrt-gemini-extension/tools/scrt-gemini.json`.
Search for the string "TODO" in the temporary verification file /tmp/scrt-verify.ts using scrt.

```tool_code
scrt_search(pattern="TODO", in=["/tmp/scrt-verify.ts"], effort="normal")
```
Save the search results from the previous step into a mind‑palace stash named "todo-verification" with a note describing its purpose.

```tool_code
scrt_stash(name="todo-verification", note="Results of searching for TODO in the temporary verification file.")
```
List all existing mind‑palace stashes to see what has been captured so far.

```tool_code
scrt_list_stashes()
```
Show me all files in the current directory that scrt can search.
scrt --ls
I want to prune any expired scratch stashes and then list the remaining ones in JSON format.
scrt --mp-prune-expired && scrt --mp-list --json
Generate a tool specification for Gemini in JSON format so I can use it as an extension.
scrt tool-spec --format gemini > scrt-gemini-extension/tools/scrt-gemini.json
What happens if I run the CLI with `--pattern-file` but no following argument?
The parser will return a ParseError with the message "Missing value for --pattern-file".
How does the parser handle a comma‑separated list after `--in`?
It splits the string on commas, discards empty entries, and adds each part to the `in_paths` vector.
Search the source tree for occurrences of the function name `parseArgs` within Rust files under the `scrt-cli/src` directory.

```tool_code
scrt_search(pattern="\\bparseArgs\\b", in=["C:\\Users\\atooz\\Programming\\ai-utils-memory\\scrt-cli\\src"], effort="normal")
```
Save the search results from the previous query into a mind‑palace slot named `arg-parser` with a note describing its purpose and tag it as `rust`, `cli`, `search`.

```tool_code
scrt_stash(name="arg-parser", note="Occurrences of the parseArgs function in the scrt-cli source code.", tags=["rust","cli","search"], replace=false)
```
Retrieve the `arg-parser` stash including the captured node bodies so that we can inspect the exact lines where parseArgs is referenced.

```tool_code
scrt_get_stash(name="arg-parser", with_nodes=true)
```
Search for the pattern "error" in files foo.txt and bar.log, ignoring case and showing only the first 10 matches.
scrt --pattern "error" -i foo.txt bar.log --ignore-case --max-nodes 10
List all mind palace stashes tagged with "project" and replace their contents with new data from stash "drafts", then compose a new stash named "final".
scrt --mp-list-tags project --mp-stash-replace --mp-from drafts --mp-compose final
Serve the CLI over HTTP on port 8080, listening only on localhost, and enable verbose output for debugging.
scrt --serve-http --serve-port 8080 --serve-host localhost
What are the two data boundaries that scrt guarantees to be lossless?
scrt guarantees that the palace file format (on‑disk JSON) and the agent‑json envelope round‑trip losslessly.
Show how to call the scrt search tool using OpenAI function calling syntax.
What field order does scrt use for JSON output and why?
scrt orders each struct’s fields to match the v0.x object literal so that `--format json` / `agent-json` produce a byte‑equal diff, matching insertion/declaration order.
Search the codebase for all occurrences of the string `scrt_similar` in TypeScript files under src/tools.

```tool_code
scrt_search(pattern="scrt_similar", in=["src/tools/**/*.ts"], effort="normal")
```
Save the search results from the previous query into a mind‑palace stash named `similar-tool-search` with a note describing its purpose.

```tool_code
scrt_stash(name="similar-tool-search", note="Search for occurrences of scrt_similar in src/tools to identify potential usage patterns.")
```
List all existing mind‑palace stashes that have the tag `search`.

```tool_code
scrt_list_stashes(tag_filter=["search"])
```
Generate the tool specification in JSON format
scrt tool-spec --format json
Output an agent-json envelope for a search result
scrt agent-json
Run the scrt_similar tool to find similar code snippets matching a pattern
scrt scrt_similar --pattern "TODO"
What does the `effort` parameter control in the search tool?

`effort` sets a preset that controls per-node context windows and node caps: 'scan' = 20t/100k-nodes, 'normal' = 500t/30n (default), 'deep' = 2000t/100n. It can be overridden with `max_tokens` or `max_nodes`.
Explain how to use the `window_curve` option when searching.

`window_curve` determines token-window decay across ranked nodes. Options are 'flat' (every node gets full window), 'linear' (decays to ~10% at last rank, good with sort:recent for budget efficiency), and 'log' (gentler decay via full/log2(rank+2)).
What is the purpose of `mp_stash` in the search tool?
`mp_stash` saves the search result into a mind-palace stash with the given name, optionally tagging it and setting an auto‑expiry via `mp_tag` and `mp_ttl`.
Find all occurrences of the regex pattern `scrt_\w+` within the Rust source files under `src/tool_spec.rs` and its subdirectories, returning only the most recent edits.

```tool_code
scrt_search(pattern="scrt_\\\\w+", in=["C:\\Users\\atooz\\Programming\\ai-utils-memory\\scrt-cli\\crates\\scrt-core\\src"], sort="recent", window_curve="linear")
```
Retrieve the stash `search-scrt-tool-spec` with node bodies to review the captured code snippets.

```tool_code
scrt_get_stash(name="search-scrt-tool-spec", with_nodes=true)
```
Search for the word "TODO" in all Rust files under src and save the results to a mind‑palace stash named "todo‑list" with a one‑day TTL.
scrt search --pattern "TODO" --in "src/**/*.rs" --mp_stash todo-list --mp_ttl 1d
Run ripgrep for the regex "fn\s+main" in the current directory, limit to 200 tokens and 5 nodes, sort by recent edits, and use a linear window curve.
scrt search --pattern "fn\\s+main" --in "." --max_tokens 200 --max_nodes 5 --sort recent --window_curve linear
Fetch and search the URL https://example.com/docs for the phrase "API endpoint", paginate results page 2 with 20 nodes per page.
scrt search --url https://example.com/docs --pattern "API endpoint" --page 2 --page_size 20
How do I build the scrt binary from source?
Clone the repository, change into the directory, then run `cargo build --release`. The binary will be located at `target/release/scrt`.
Generate a tool specification for Claude Code in anthropic format and place it inside a plugin directory.
Run `scrt tool-spec --format anthropic > scrt-plugin/tools/scrt-anthropic.json` after creating the `scrt-plugin/tools/` directory.
What command verifies that scrt is installed correctly?
Run `scrt --version`, `scrt --help`, or `scrt --ls` to confirm the binary works and lists files in the current working directory.
Search the repository for any usage of the `--serve` flag in the installation guide.

```tool_code
scrt_search(pattern="\\-\\-serve", in=["C:\\Users\\atooz\\Programming\\ai-utils-memory\\scrt-cli"], effort="normal")
```
Save the search results of `--serve` into a mind‑palace slot named "serve-usage" with a note describing its purpose.

```tool_code
scrt_stash(name="serve-usage", note="Occurrences of the scrt --serve flag in the install guide", tags=["install","flag"])
```
List all stashes that have the tag "install" to see what other installation‑related snippets are stored.

```tool_code
scrt_list_stashes(tag_filter=["install"])
```
Generate a Claude Code compatible tool specification for scrt.
scrt tool-spec --format anthropic
Run a quick smoke test by searching for the word "TODO" in a temporary TypeScript file and output the result as JSON.
scrt "TODO" --in /tmp/scrt-verify.ts --format json | grep '"status"'
Start scrt as a warm stdio server to handle multiple calls from an agent.
scrt --serve
How do I specify multiple values for the `--mp-compose` flag in the CLI?
After writing `--mp-compose`, provide one or more arguments that do not start with a dash; each will be split on commas and added to the `mp_compose` list. For example: `--mp-compose foo,bar baz`.
Parse the command line arguments for the `--mp-except` flag.

The parser increments the index, sets `mp_except` to the next argument (e.g., `foo`), then continues consuming subsequent non‑flag arguments as names into `mp_except_names`. Each consumed token is comma‑split before being added.
What happens when I use the flag `--mp-prune-keep 10`?
The parser reads the integer `10`, parses it with `parse_int`, and stores it in `mp_prune_keep`. This value is used later to determine how many items to keep during pruning.


Show the contents of the stash named "project_notes" and include only nodes tagged with "meeting" or "action".
scrt --mp-get project_notes --mp-list-tags meeting,action
Add a new entry to the stash "ideas" with note text "Brainstorm UI improvements" and tags "ui", "design".
scrt --mp-stash-name ideas --mp-stash-note "Brainstorm UI improvements" --mp-stash-tags ui,design
Compose the mind palace from stashes "project_notes" and "ideas", then intersect with stash "meeting_minutes" and exclude nodes named "draft".
scrt --mp-compose project_notes,ideas --mp-intersect meeting_minutes --mp-except draft
How do I use the `--mp-graph` flag to specify a graph name with an optional depth?
Use `--mp-graph <name> [depth]`. The `<name>` is required, and if you provide a second argument that does not start with '-', it will be parsed as the depth (defaulting to 3).
What values are allowed for the `--score` flag and how is it validated?
`--score` must be an integer between 1 and 10 inclusive. The parser checks that the value falls within this range and returns a ParseError otherwise.

scrt --in /var/log/**/*.log -p error --ignore-case --fuzzy --before 0 --after 0 --max-nodes 10

scrt --mp-list --mp-list-tags urgent --mp-get meeting_minutes --mp-get-with-nodes --mp-drop old_notes
How do I specify the type for a Mind Palace link using the CLI?
Use the `--mp-link <type>` flag followed by the optional note argument if desired.
Parse the `--mp-graph` option with an optional depth value.
The parser reads the name after `--mp-graph`, then checks if the next token is not a flag; if so, it parses that token as an integer depth (defaulting to 3).
What range of values is allowed for the `--score` option?
The `--score` value must be between 1 and 10 inclusive.
